skill.name: database-schema-design
skill.description: Design normalized SQL schemas, NoSQL data models, relationships, indexes, constraints, and migration plans for database-backed applications.
skill.summary: Design practical SQL and NoSQL database schemas with relationships, indexes, constraints, and migration guidance.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found documentation-only Markdown examples, reference links, and relative skill links. No executable code, command runner, data exfiltration flow, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md or SKILL.toon.

low_findings.title: Static command alerts are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside fenced Markdown, SQL, Mermaid, or inline documentation examples. They are not executed by the skill and do not create a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are static Markdown examples or inline text, not runnable scripts. No shell invocation, interpreter wrapper, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs are documentation links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to official database documentation, modeling tools, and learning resources. The skill does not instruct automated fetching or sending user data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are a References section with ordinary Markdown hyperlinks. There is no code path that performs network access or exfiltrates data.

low_findings.title: Relative paths are cross-skill documentation links
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections are relative Markdown links to related skills. They do not read files or instruct the model to access parent directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only path traversal sequences appear in Markdown link targets. No filesystem API, command, or request asks the agent to open these paths.

low_findings.title: Cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are keyword matches
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections match schema terminology, identifiers, and example field names such as password_hash. No weak algorithm implementation, secret collection, or host reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are database design vocabulary and example column names. The files contain no executable cryptographic code and no system inspection instructions.

low_findings.title: Migration examples include destructive rollback SQL
low_findings.description: The skill includes down migration examples that drop tables with CASCADE. This is legitimate migration documentation but can cause data loss if copied without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SQL rollback example is intentionally destructive, although it is not executed by the skill. The risk depends on users manually applying generated migrations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive SQL rollback example
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation shows rollback SQL using DROP TABLE with CASCADE. Users should review generated migrations and test rollbacks before applying them to production databases.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The statement is visible in an example migration and could remove data if reused. It is documentation-only and does not run automatically.

content.value_statement: Database projects often fail when entities, relationships, and indexes are designed too late. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured schema design for SQL and NoSQL systems.
actual_capabilities: Identifies entities, attributes, primary keys, and foreign keys from requirements.
Designs normalized SQL schemas for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and similar databases.
Models MongoDB collections with embedding, referencing, and index tradeoffs.
Suggests indexes, constraints, triggers, and integrity rules for common access patterns.
Outlines migration file structure, rollback plans, ERD documentation, and schema notes.
Explains normalization, denormalization, and performance tradeoffs for read and write workloads.
limitations: It does not inspect a live database or measure real query plans by itself.
It cannot guarantee production safety without workload data and human review.
Generated migration examples must be tested before use on real data.
It provides design guidance, not vendor-specific compliance certification.

use_cases.title: Plan a new product database
use_cases.description: Turn product requirements into tables, relationships, indexes, and migration notes before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Refactor a slow schema
use_cases.description: Review entity boundaries, indexes, denormalization choices, and foreign keys for a growing application.

use_cases.title: Document data models for review
use_cases.description: Create ERD-ready descriptions and schema documentation for technical planning or architecture review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new application database.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PostgreSQL schema for [application type]. Entities are [entities]. Include primary keys, foreign keys, required fields, and basic indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Add relationships and constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with known business rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a schema for [domain] with these rules: [rules]. Explain one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships. Include constraints and delete behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize for workload
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this schema idea for [database]. Workload is [read/write ratio], data volume is [volume], and common queries are [queries]. Recommend indexes and normalization changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan migrations and documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration and documentation plan for this schema change: [change]. Include rollout order, rollback concerns, data safety checks, and ERD documentation notes.

output_examples.input: A marketplace needs users, vendors, products, orders, payments, and reviews.
output: A normalized entity list with ownership boundaries and required attributes.
Relationship notes for vendors, products, orders, line items, payments, and reviews.
Index recommendations for product search, order history, payment lookup, and review moderation.

output_examples.input: A chat app needs fast conversation reads and message history retrieval.
output: A MongoDB collection plan with users, conversations, and messages.
Denormalization guidance for participant summaries and last message previews.
Index suggestions for conversation membership, recent messages, and sender lookup.

output_examples.input: An existing orders table is growing and status queries are slow.
output: A review of likely missing indexes and query access patterns.
Composite index recommendations based on status and creation time.
Warnings about excess indexes and migration testing for large tables.

best_practices: Provide entities, expected data volume, common queries, and read/write ratio before asking for a schema.
Ask for indexes and constraints together, because integrity and performance decisions affect each other.
Review generated migrations with backups, staging tests, and rollback plans before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a schema without describing relationships and ownership rules.
Do not copy destructive rollback migrations into production without data safety review.
Do not denormalize only for convenience without explaining the query workload.

faq.question: Does this skill support both SQL and NoSQL databases?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers SQL schema design and MongoDB-style document modeling with tradeoff explanations.

faq.question: Can it generate migration plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline migration order, rollback concerns, and documentation needs. You must test migrations before use.

faq.question: Will it connect to my database?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation guidance and does not connect to live databases by itself.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing schema?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current tables, common queries, data volume, and performance problems for useful recommendations.

faq.question: Does it create ERD diagrams?
faq.answer: It can describe ERD structure and Mermaid-ready relationships, but rendering depends on your editor or tool.

faq.question: Is generated SQL ready for production?
faq.answer: No. Treat generated SQL as a draft and review it with database backups, staging tests, and operational checks.
